requirements.title: Support multiple question types and tagging
requirements.description: The system shall support creation of multiple choice, drag-and-drop, and code snippet questions.
requirements.rationale: To enable instructors to create diverse assessments that accurately test competencies with appropriate difficulty levels.
requirements.business_value: Improves the effectiveness of assessments by providing a variety of question types and difficulty tiers, thereby better aligning with learning objectives.

source.description: As an instructor I need an exam builder that supports multiple choice, drag-and-drop, and code snippet questions

acceptance_criteria: I can assign one or more competencies, a difficulty tier from Beginner, Intermediate, or Advanced, and a nugget reward multiplier to that question
preconditions: The system is running and accessible.
An exam creator is logged in.
postconditions: A new question of the specified type has been created with the assigned competencies, difficulty tier, and nugget reward multiplier.
functional_constraints: The system must support multiple choice, drag-and-drop, and code snippet questions.
Each question can be tagged with one or more competencies.
Each question must have a difficulty tier from Beginner, Intermediate, or Advanced.
Each question can have an associated nugget reward multiplier.
edge_cases: What happens if a question is created without any competencies?
Can a question be tagged with multiple difficulty tiers simultaneously?
How does the system handle invalid or incorrect input for competencies, difficulty tiers, and nugget reward multipliers?

interfaces.name: Question Creation API
interfaces.description: Used by the LMS to create new questions with specified details.

interfaces.name: Exam Builder Database
interfaces.description: Stores information about created questions, including their type, competencies, difficulty tiers, and nugget reward multipliers.

test_cases.description: Create a multiple choice question with one competency and Beginner difficulty.
test_cases.expected_result: A new multiple choice question is created and saved in the database with the specified competency and difficulty.

test_cases.description: Attempt to create a question without specifying any competencies.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should return an error message indicating that at least one competency must be specified.

requirements.title: Create Student Profile During Registration
requirements.description: The system shall automatically create a student account upon successful registration with a default avatar, zero nugget balance, no badges, and an empty competency radar chart.
requirements.rationale: To ensure that each student has a personalized learning experience from the start, allowing for tracking of progress and customization of their profile.
requirements.business_value: Improves user satisfaction by providing a customized and intuitive interface that reflects individual needs and achievements.

source.description: As a Student, I need to register and have a personalized profile including an avatar, nugget balance, earned badges, and a competency radar chart, so that I can track my progress and have a customized learning experience.

acceptance_criteria: A student account is created upon successful registration.
The profile is generated with a default avatar, zero nuggets, no badges, and an empty competency radar chart.
postconditions: The registered student sees their personalized profile including the avatar, current nugget balance, earned badges, and competency radar chart.
The system updates the profile based on platform activities.
functional_constraints: The registration process collects necessary user information and creates a profile automatically.
Avatar can be customized during or after registration.

non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should be intuitive and user-friendly.

edge_cases: User provides invalid input during registration.
System fails to create a student account upon successful registration due to internal error.
Avatar customization request is received after the profile has been created.

interfaces.name: Registration API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to create a student account and generate a personalized profile.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this database to store user information, avatar details, nugget balance, badges, and competency radar chart data.

interfaces.name: Profile UI
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this interface to display the personalized profile of the student.

test_cases.description: Verify that a student account is created upon successful registration.
test_cases.expected_result: A new student account is created with default values: default avatar, zero nuggets, no badges, and an empty competency radar chart.

test_cases.description: Verify that the profile page displays the correct information.
test_cases.expected_result: The profile page shows the student's current avatar, nugget balance, earned badges, and competency radar chart.

test_cases.description: Verify that an invalid input during registration does not create a student account.
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed to the user, indicating that the input is invalid.

requirements.title: Schedule Exams with Date Range and Optional Time Limit
requirements.description: The system shall allow an instructor to schedule exams within a specified open date and close date, with an optional per-attempt time limit enforced server-side.
requirements.rationale: Enabling instructors to control the availability and duration of exams ensures fair assessment conditions for students.
requirements.business_value: Improves exam integrity by preventing students from accessing exams outside designated times or attempting them without proper constraints.

source.description: As a instructor I need to schedule exams with an open date, close date, and optional per-attempt time limit enforced server-side so that students can only access the exam within the specified time window and are limited to the allowed time per attempt.

acceptance_criteria: Given an exam is scheduled with an open date and a close date, When a student tries to access the exam outside that time window, Then the exam is not accessible and an appropriate message is displayed

Given an exam is scheduled with a per-attempt time limit of 60 minutes, When a student starts an attempt, Then the server records the start time and begins counting down the 60-minute limit, And when the time limit expires, the attempt is automatically submitted
Given an exam is scheduled with no per-attempt time limit, When a student starts an attempt, Then the student can take as much time as needed within the open/close window
preconditions: The exam has been created in the system
The instructor has inputted the open and close dates for the exam
The instructor has optionally set a per-attempt time limit for the exam
postconditions: Students are only able to access the exam during the specified open date and close date
If a per-attempt time limit is set, students' attempts must be submitted within that time limit
If no per-attempt time limit is set, students can take as much time as needed within the open/close window
functional_constraints: The system must enforce the scheduled open and close dates for exam access
The system must record and enforce the start time of each student attempt if a per-attempt time limit is set
The system must automatically submit an attempt when the per-attempt time limit expires
security_requirements: Access to the exam should be restricted outside of the scheduled open and close dates
Student attempts should not be tampered with on the client side

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must handle a reasonable number of concurrent students without significant performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time to ensure reliable access and submission of exams
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to scale up as the number of students increases
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should clearly communicate why a student cannot access an exam or submit an attempt

edge_cases: What happens if a student tries to access an exam on the exact open date?
How does the system handle daylight saving time changes during the exam period?
What if a student's device loses internet connection during their attempt?

interfaces.name: Exam Access API
interfaces.description: Interface for checking if a student is allowed to access an exam.

interfaces.name: Attempt Submission API
interfaces.description: Interface for submitting student attempts with time tracking.

interfaces.name: Exam Database
interfaces.description: Stores exam details, access permissions, and attempt records.

test_cases.description: Student tries to access an exam on the exact open date.
test_cases.expected_result: The student is allowed to access the exam.

test_cases.description: Student attempts an exam with a 60-minute time limit and submits within the time limit.
test_cases.expected_result: The attempt is successfully submitted.

test_cases.description: Student attempts an exam with a 60-minute time limit and exceeds the time limit.
test_cases.expected_result: The attempt is automatically submitted.

test_cases.description: Student attempts an exam with no time limit set.
test_cases.expected_result: The student can take as much time as needed within the open/close window.

requirements.title: Provide Instant Competency Breakdown After Exam Submission
requirements.description: The system shall immediately analyze submitted exam results and display a competency breakdown with weak competency flags and study tips.
requirements.rationale: This requirement addresses the need for students to quickly identify their weak areas after submitting an exam, allowing them to focus on targeted studying immediately.
requirements.business_value: Enhances student learning by providing immediate feedback and tailored support, potentially improving overall academic performance.

source.description: As a student I need an instant competency breakdown after exam submission So that I can identify weak areas and get targeted study tips

acceptance_criteria: Given I have submitted an exam
When the system processes my results
Then I see a competency breakdown with weak competency flags and study tips
preconditions: An exam has been submitted by the student.
postconditions: The student can view a competency breakdown.
Weak competency areas are flagged.
Study tips corresponding to flagged weaknesses are provided.
functional_constraints: The analysis must be performed within one minute of submission.
security_requirements: Student data is protected according to institutional privacy policies.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for competency breakdown and study tips should not exceed 2 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time during operational hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must support up to 1,000 students submitting exams simultaneously without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable.

edge_cases: What happens if the exam results contain incorrect or incomplete data?
How will the system handle simultaneous submissions from multiple students?
What is the behavior when there are no weak competencies identified in the results?
dependencies: Database for Storing Exam Results and Student Data

interfaces.name: Exam Analysis API
interfaces.description: Interface used to submit exam results and receive a competency breakdown.

interfaces.name: Student Dashboard
interfaces.description: Interface where students can view their competency breakdown and study tips.

test_cases.description: Student submits an exam with some weak competencies.
test_cases.expected_result: System processes the results, identifies weak competencies, and displays them with corresponding study tips on the student's dashboard.

test_cases.description: Student submits an exam without any weak competencies.
test_cases.expected_result: System processes the results and indicates that all competencies are strong, with no study tips provided.

requirements.title: Cohort Creation and Management
requirements.description: The system shall allow an administrator to create new cohorts, ensuring that each cohort is saved and made available for student enrollment.
requirements.rationale: Enabling administrators to organize students into cohorts is essential for efficient management and control over exam access.
requirements.business_value: Improves the organization of students into meaningful groups, facilitating better management and control over exam access.

source.description: As a administrator I need to create cohorts, enroll individual students, and restrict exams to one or more cohorts so that I can manage student groups and control exam access efficiently.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am an administrator, When I create a new cohort, Then the cohort is saved and available for enrollment
Given I have a cohort, When I enroll an individual student, Then the student is added to that cohort
Given I have an exam and one or more cohorts, When I restrict the exam to those cohorts, Then only students in those cohorts can access the exam
preconditions: The system must be operational and accessible.
The administrator must have the necessary permissions.
postconditions: A new cohort is created and saved in the system.
An individual student is successfully enrolled in a cohort.
The exam access restriction is applied correctly to specified cohorts.
functional_constraints: Cohorts must be unique and non-duplicate entries.
security_requirements: Access control measures should ensure that only authorized administrators can create, modify, and restrict exams.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should respond within a reasonable time frame for cohort creation, enrollment, and exam restriction processes.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available at least 99.9% of the time to ensure minimal disruptions in administration operations.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to support an increasing number of cohorts and students without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, guiding administrators through common issues during cohort creation and exam restriction.

edge_cases: What happens when a duplicate cohort is attempted to be created?
How does the system handle a scenario where an exam is restricted to a non-existent cohort?
What if a student tries to access an exam that is not restricted but still cannot due to other restrictions?
How should the system respond when there are multiple overlapping restrictions for an exam?

interfaces.name: Cohort API
interfaces.description: Interface for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting cohorts.

interfaces.name: Student Cohort Database
interfaces.description: Database to store cohort information and student enrollments.

interfaces.name: Exam Restriction API
interfaces.description: Interface for restricting exams to specific cohorts.

test_cases.description: Create a new cohort with valid input.
test_cases.expected_result: Cohort is created successfully and is available for enrollment.

test_cases.description: Enroll an individual student in an existing cohort.
test_cases.expected_result: Student is added to the cohort successfully.

test_cases.description: Restrict an exam to one or more valid cohorts.
test_cases.expected_result: Exam access is restricted correctly for specified cohorts.

test_cases.description: Attempt to create a duplicate cohort.
test_cases.expected_result: Error message indicating that the cohort already exists.

test_cases.description: Restrict an exam to non-existent cohorts.
test_cases.expected_result: Error message indicating that one or more specified cohorts do not exist.

requirements.title: Single Question Per Screen Exam Interface
requirements.description: The system shall present exams one question at a time with a game-like interface, live timer, progress bar, and animated nugget counter.
requirements.rationale: To enhance student engagement, track progress, and provide motivation throughout the exam experience.
requirements.business_value: Improves exam completion rates by keeping students engaged and motivated.

source.description: As a student I need to have exams delivered one question at a time with a game-like interface, live timer, progress bar, and animated nugget counter so that I can stay engaged, track my progress, and feel motivated throughout the exam.

acceptance_criteria: I see one question at a time with a game-like interface, a live timer, a progress bar, and an animated nugget counter
The next question appears automatically and the progress bar updates when I answer and submit
The current answer is submitted and the exam ends when the timer reaches zero
The nugget counter plays an animation when it updates
preconditions: I am taking an exam
postconditions: One question at a time with game-like interface, live timer, progress bar, and animated nugget counter is visible
Progress bar accurately reflects completion percentage or question count
Current answer is submitted automatically when I submit
Exam ends when the timer reaches zero
Nugget counter animation plays upon earning a nugget
functional_constraints: Questions are presented in a single-question-per-screen format
Game-like interface includes visual elements and animations
Live timer counts down or up as appropriate
Progress bar shows completion percentage or question count
Animated nugget counter rewards correct answers or milestones
edge_cases: What happens when a student skips a question without answering?
How does the system handle network interruptions during an exam?
What if a student tries to manipulate the timer or progress bar?

interfaces.name: Exam Interface
interfaces.description: The user interface that displays the exam questions, game-like elements, live timer, progress bar, and nugget counter.

interfaces.name: Question Service API
interfaces.description: An API that provides the next question to be displayed during the exam.

interfaces.name: Progress Tracking Database
interfaces.description: A database that stores the student's progress and nugget counts throughout the exam.

test_cases.description: Student starts an exam.
test_cases.expected_result: The exam begins with the first question displayed on the screen, along with a game-like interface, live timer, progress bar, and animated nugget counter.

test_cases.description: Student answers and submits a question.
test_cases.expected_result: The next question appears automatically, the progress bar updates to reflect the current progress, and no further questions appear until all are answered.

test_cases.description: Timer reaches zero during an exam.
test_cases.expected_result: The current answer is submitted, the exam ends, and the final results are displayed to the student.

test_cases.description: Student earns a nugget.
test_cases.expected_result: The nugget counter updates, and an animated celebration plays to motivate the student.

requirements.title: Streak System Implementation
requirements.description: The system shall implement a streak system where consecutive correct answers apply a nugget multiplier (1.5×, 2×, 3×) that resets on a wrong answer.
requirements.rationale: To encourage players to maintain consecutive correct answers and increase their earnings, thereby enhancing engagement and satisfaction within the game.
requirements.business_value: Increased player retention and loyalty due to higher rewards for sustained performance, leading to potential long-term revenue growth.

source.description: As a player I need a streak system where consecutive correct answers apply a nugget multiplier (1.5×, 2×, 3×) that resets on a wrong answer So that I can earn increased nuggets for maintaining a streak

acceptance_criteria: Given I am answering a question, When I answer correctly for the first time, Then I receive base nuggets (no multiplier)
Given I have answered correctly once, When I answer correctly again, Then I receive nuggets multiplied by 1.5×
Given I have answered correctly twice consecutively, When I answer correctly a third time, Then I receive nuggets multiplied by 2×
Given I have answered correctly three times consecutively, When I answer correctly a fourth time, Then I receive nuggets multiplied by 3×
Given I have a streak of at least one correct answer, When I answer incorrectly, Then my streak resets and the multiplier returns to 1×
preconditions: The player is actively answering questions within the game environment.
postconditions: If the player answers correctly, they receive nuggets multiplied according to their current streak.
If the player answers incorrectly, their streak resets and the multiplier reverts to 1×.
functional_constraints: The system must track consecutive correct answers accurately.
The multiplier should start at 1× and increase by increments of 0.5× for each additional correct answer up to a maximum of 3×.
The streak resets to 1× on any incorrect answer.

performance: Responsiveness: The system should respond within 200ms during normal operation.
availability: Uptime: At least 99.5% uptime during peak hours.
scalability: The system must handle a minimum of 1,000 concurrent players without performance degradation.
usability: Error handling: Provide clear feedback to the player if their answer is incorrect.
Interface behavior: Clearly display the current streak and multiplier value.

edge_cases: What happens if a player logs out and then logs back in? Does their streak continue?
How does the system handle extremely high streaks (e.g., more than three consecutive correct answers)?
Can the multiplier be adjusted by administrators or is it fixed as described?
dependencies: Question Management System: To provide questions for players to answer.

interfaces.name: Question API
interfaces.description: Interacts with the Question Management System to fetch and validate player answers.

interfaces.name: Player Data Database
interfaces.description: Stores player streak information and multiplier values.

interfaces.name: Game Interface
interfaces.description: Displays the current streak and multiplier to the player.

test_cases.description: Player answers a question correctly for the first time.
test_cases.expected_result: Player receives base nuggets with no multiplier applied.

test_cases.description: Player answers three questions consecutively correctly.
test_cases.expected_result: Player receives nuggets multiplied by 3× for the third correct answer.

test_cases.description: Player answers a question incorrectly after a streak of three consecutive correct answers.
test_cases.expected_result: Streak resets to 1×, multiplier returns to 1×.

requirements.title: Student should be able to redeem nuggets for rewards
requirements.description: The system shall allow a student to redeem nuggets from their wallet for virtual items or instructor-configured institutional perks in the reward store.
requirements.rationale: Enabling students to redeem nuggets ensures that they can spend their earned rewards on meaningful items or benefits, enhancing engagement and motivation.
requirements.business_value: Increasing student engagement by providing tangible incentives for desired behaviors or achievements.

source.description: As a student I need a nugget wallet redeemable in a reward store for virtual items or instructor-configured institutional perks So that I can spend my earned nuggets on meaningful rewards

acceptance_criteria: Given a student has earned nuggets in their wallet, When the student selects a reward item in the store, Then the nugget balance is reduced by the item's cost, And the student receives the reward (virtual item unlock or perk applied)
Given a student attempts to redeem an item without enough nuggets, When the student selects that item, Then the redemption is denied, And a message is shown indicating insufficient nuggets
preconditions: The student has earned nuggets in their wallet
The reward store contains items available for redemption
postconditions: The student's nugget balance is reduced by the cost of the redeemed item
The selected reward item is provided to the student
security_requirements: Authentication and authorization checks are performed before allowing a student to redeem nuggets
Redemption requests are validated against available nugget balance
edge_cases: What happens if a student attempts to redeem an item that is not available in the reward store?
How does the system handle negative nugget balances if a bug or error causes one?
What if the reward store administrator changes the cost of an item after a student has earned but before redemption?

interfaces.name: Reward Store API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to retrieve available reward items and update the student's nugget balance.

interfaces.name: Student Dashboard
interfaces.description: The requirement is implemented through the user interface, allowing students to select and redeem reward items.

interfaces.name: Instructor Configuration System
interfaces.description: This system provides instructor-configured institutional perks that can be redeemed with nuggets.

test_cases.description: A student earns nuggets and attempts to redeem a virtual item.
test_cases.expected_result: The student's nugget balance is reduced by the item's cost, and the virtual item is unlocked for the student.

test_cases.description: A student earns nuggets but attempts to redeem an item that costs more than their current balance.
test_cases.expected_result: The redemption is denied, and a message indicating insufficient nuggets is displayed.

test_cases.description: A student attempts to redeem an item from a non-existent reward store.
test_cases.expected_result: The redemption is denied, and an error message indicating the issue is displayed.

requirements.title: Review Past Attempts and Competency Trends
requirements.description: The system shall display a chronological list of past attempts for the student, including per-question details such as the student's answer, correct answer, and learning nugget.
requirements.rationale: This requirement allows students to track their learning progress and identify areas needing improvement by reviewing past performance and competency trends over time.
requirements.business_value: Improved understanding of learning outcomes, facilitating targeted improvements in study strategies and focus on weak areas.

source.description: As a student I need to review past attempts with my answers, correct answers, nuggets per question, and competency trend charts So that I can track my learning progress and identify areas needing improvement.

acceptance_criteria: I see a chronological list of my past attempts
I see for each question: my answer, the correct answer, and a learning nugget
I see graphical trends of my performance over time for each competency
preconditions: I am a student with multiple completed attempts
Competency trend charts have sufficient data to generate trends
postconditions: The user has reviewed past attempts and identified areas needing improvement
functional_constraints: Data privacy rules must be adhered to when displaying past attempts and competency trends
security_requirements: Authentication must be required before accessing the review section
Data should be encrypted in transit and at rest to protect student information

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should respond within 2 seconds for a typical user session
non_functional_requirements.availability: 99.9% uptime is expected with minimal downtime during peak times
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle at least 10,000 students simultaneously without significant performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and concise to guide the user on how to correct their actions

edge_cases: What happens if a student has no past attempts?
How should the system handle data privacy concerns when displaying competency trends?
What if there is an error in calculating competency trend charts?
dependencies: Data Storage (for storing past attempts and competency data)
Graphing Library (for generating competency trend charts)

interfaces.name: Student Dashboard
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the student's dashboard to provide access to the review section.

interfaces.name: LMS API
interfaces.description: The requirement uses the LMS API to fetch past attempts and competency data for the student.

interfaces.name: Student Data Store
interfaces.description: The requirement reads from a database to retrieve past attempts and competency trend data.

test_cases.description: A student with multiple completed attempts navigates to the review section.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a chronological list of past attempts for the student.

test_cases.description: A student selects a specific past attempt and views its details.
test_cases.expected_result: For each question, the system shows the student's answer, correct answer, and learning nugget.

test_cases.description: A student with insufficient attempt history accesses the competency trend charts.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an appropriate message indicating there is not enough data to generate trends.

requirements.title: Instructor Registration and Login
requirements.description: The system shall allow an instructor to create an account with required details, log in with valid credentials, and be redirected to their personal dashboard.
requirements.rationale: This requirement ensures that instructors can access the necessary tools for managing courses and monitoring student progress efficiently.
requirements.business_value: By enabling instructors to register and log in, the system enhances productivity and allows for better tracking of course enrollment and student performance.

source.description: As a instructor I need to register and log in to a personal dashboard that shows active exams, enrollment counts, and competency coverage so that I can manage my courses and monitor student progress efficiently.

acceptance_criteria: An instructor can create an account with required details when accessing the registration page.
An instructor is redirected to their personal dashboard upon logging in with valid credentials.
An instructor sees active exams, enrollment counts, and competency coverage when viewing their dashboard.
preconditions: Instructor is not registered (for registration feature).
Instructor has registered and knows valid credentials (for login feature).
Instructor is on their personal dashboard.
postconditions: An instructor's account is created with required details.
An instructor logs in successfully and is redirected to the dashboard.
The dashboard displays active exams, enrollment counts, and competency coverage.
security_requirements: Strong authentication mechanism for user accounts.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Dashboard updates in real-time or near real-time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: System should handle an increase in the number of registered instructors and students without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Clear and intuitive interface for easy navigation and data interpretation.

edge_cases: What happens if an instructor tries to register with existing credentials?
How does the system handle a sudden surge in user activity?
What actions should be taken if a user attempts to log in from an unrecognized device?

interfaces.name: Registration API
interfaces.description: Handles the creation of new instructor accounts.

interfaces.name: Login API
interfaces.description: Authenticates user credentials and returns a session token.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: Stores user information including login details and dashboard data.

interfaces.name: Dashboard UI
interfaces.description: Displays active exams, enrollment counts, and competency coverage.

test_cases.description: Instructor tries to register with existing credentials.
test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message indicating that the account already exists.

test_cases.description: User attempts to log in from an unrecognized device.
test_cases.expected_result: System prompts for a second factor authentication or blocks access after several failed attempts.

requirements.title: Cohort Leaderboard Ranked by Nuggets
requirements.description: The system shall provide a cohort leaderboard that ranks students based on the total nuggets earned within a configurable time window.
requirements.rationale: This requirement allows students to track their progress and compare it with peers, fostering a sense of competition and motivation.
requirements.business_value: Enhances student engagement and performance by providing visibility into ranking and progress.

source.description: As a student, I need a cohort leaderboard ranked by nuggets over a configurable window so that I can track my progress and see how I compare with peers.

acceptance_criteria: Given the leaderboard is enabled by the instructor
And I have not opted out
When I view the cohort leaderboard
Then I see myself and other students ranked by total nuggets earned in the configured time window
preconditions: The leaderboard feature is enabled for the student's cohort
The student has not opted out of the leaderboard
postconditions: Student sees their rank based on total nuggets earned within the configured time window
Student sees ranks of other students in the cohort based on total nuggets earned within the configured time window
functional_constraints: Leaderboard data is aggregated over a configurable time window
Students can opt out of appearing on the leaderboard
Instructors can disable the entire leaderboard for the cohort

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system shall respond within 2 seconds to display the leaderboard.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The leaderboard must be available at least 99.5% of the time during regular operations.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system shall support up to 10,000 students on the leaderboard simultaneously without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable; the interface should be intuitive and easy to navigate.

edge_cases: What happens when the configured time window is set to a period where no nuggets have been earned?
How does the system handle changes in the time window while students are viewing the leaderboard?
dependencies: Student accounts and their nugget data

interfaces.name: Student Data Store
interfaces.description: The system retrieves student nugget data from this database to populate the leaderboard.

interfaces.name: Cohort Settings API
interfaces.description: This API is used to check if the leaderboard is enabled and retrieve the current time window configuration.

interfaces.name: Leaderboard Display
interfaces.description: The user interface displays the leaderboard based on data fetched from the database and settings from the API.

test_cases.description: Verify that the leaderboard is displayed when the feature is enabled by the instructor.
test_cases.expected_result: The leaderboard should be visible to students who have not opted out.

test_cases.description: Check the functionality of the configurable time window on the leaderboard.
test_cases.expected_result: Students should see their rank based on nuggets earned within the configured time window.

requirements.title: Manual Bonus Nugget Granting with Justification
requirements.description: The system shall allow a teacher to manually grant bonus nuggets to individual students and record the justification for each grant as an audit event.
requirements.rationale: This requirement is necessary to enable teachers to reward students appropriately while maintaining a record of all grants for auditing purposes.
requirements.business_value: It enhances the flexibility of grading by allowing teachers to recognize exceptional performance and ensures accountability in the distribution of bonus nuggets.

source.description: As a teacher I need to grant manual bonus nuggets to individual students with a written justification so that the justification is logged as an audit event for record-keeping.

acceptance_criteria: A teacher is logged into the system
The teacher grants a manual bonus nugget to a student with a written justification
The bonus nugget is added to the student's account
An audit event is created containing the teacher, student, amount, and justification
preconditions: A teacher is logged into the system
postconditions: The bonus nugget is added to the student's account
An audit event is created containing the teacher, student, amount, and justification
edge_cases: Teacher attempts to grant a bonus nugget without a justification
Non-teaching staff tries to access the bonus granting feature
System fails to create an audit event due to database issues

interfaces.name: Teacher Dashboard
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the user interface where teachers can grant bonus nuggets and provide justifications.

interfaces.name: Student Account API
interfaces.description: The requirement uses an API to update a student's account balance after granting a bonus nugget.

interfaces.name: Audit Logging API
interfaces.description: The requirement sends data to an audit logging system to record each bonus grant with the teacher, student, amount, and justification.

test_cases.description: Teacher grants a manual bonus nugget with a written justification.
test_cases.expected_result: The bonus nugget is added to the student's account, and an audit event is created containing the teacher, student, amount, and justification.

test_cases.description: Teacher attempts to grant a bonus nugget without a justification.
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message and prevents the bonus from being granted.

test_cases.description: Non-teaching staff tries to access the bonus granting feature.
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies access and prompts the user with a permission denied message.
